name: Endeavour Silver Corp.

profile.description: Endeavour Silver Corp. is a mid-tier silver mining company that acquires, explores, develops, and operates silver-gold mines in the Americas. Operations include the Guanacevi and Bolanitos mines in Mexico, the newly operational Terronera mine in Mexico, and the recently acquired Kolpa mine in Peru. The company also explores for precious metals and polymetals. Founded in 1981 and headquartered in Vancouver, Canada.

innovation.label: DEVELOPING
innovation.analysis: Track Record (2/3): Endeavour Silver executed two transformative acquisitions in 2025-2026 — bringing the Terronera mine into production and acquiring the Kolpa mine in Peru — which doubled production capacity to nearly 2M oz silver and 12K oz gold quarterly, with base metals adding 3M silver-equivalent ounces in Q1 2026, a 78% production increase YoY [1]. These represent successful operational pivots that opened major new revenue streams. AI Deployment (0/2): No evidence of AI deployment or strategy in Endeavour Silver's operations. The company remains a traditional mining operation without reported AI-driven cost savings or products. Market Share (1/2): Endeavour is a meaningful mid-tier silver miner but not a category leader. With Q1 2026 revenue of $210M and 2026 guidance of 14.6-15.6M silver-equivalent ounces, it is a growing player but does not dominate the silver mining space [1]. Product Pipeline (1/2): 2026 guidance calls for 14.6-15.6M silver-equivalent ounces at AISC of $27-28/oz, providing clear production visibility. The Terronera and Kolpa ramp-ups offer specific growth paths [1]. R&D Intensity (1/1): While mining companies don't have traditional R&D, Endeavour invests in exploration programs and mine development that represent above-average reinvestment relative to mid-tier silver mining peers.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): Endeavour Silver's purpose centers on responsible mining to create value for shareholders and communities. The company introduced its ICARE Values program to articulate 'The Endeavour Way,' which is a clear but not transformative purpose — it identifies what the company does and for whom without a deeper vision of a better future [2]. Community Impact (1/2): Endeavour has a 10-year track record of ESG reporting. Operations in Mexico and Peru include community development programs and environmental protection integrated into mine management. Board member Angela Johnson was appointed for her ESG expertise [3]. However, no specific charitable dollar amounts or distinctive programs were identified. Employee Retention (1/2): CEO Dan Dickson has emphasized internal culture and safety performance since taking the role in 2021. The ICARE Values program suggests intentional culture-building. With 2,160 employees across multiple countries, the company maintains stable operations [2]. Insider Buying (1/2): CEO Dan Dickson maintains equity alignment through compensation structure, though no significant net insider buying was identified in recent filings.

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored on available metrics. Q1 2026 was transformative — revenue surged 230% YoY to $210M driven by Terronera and Kolpa additions, mine operating earnings reached $93.5M, and EBITDA hit $112.6M [1]. The 3-year revenue CAGR of 30.5% (2022 $210M to 2025 $467.5M) reflects strong growth momentum. Debt-to-equity of 39% is well below the 70% target, and forward P/E of 6.07 is attractive at well below the 20 target. However, trailing ROE remains negative at -3.9% as the company transitions from loss to profitability, and profit margin of -3.5% reflects the prior year's losses before Terronera/Kolpa contributions [1]. The forward earnings estimate of $1.32/share suggests profitability has arrived, but trailing metrics still reflect the pre-production phase. Growth stock classification applies as no dividend is paid.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (8/9): Silver mining is structurally immune to AI disruption. The core business — extracting silver and gold from underground mines in Mexico and Peru — involves physical-world operations (drilling, blasting, ore processing, environmental remediation) that AI cannot replace [1]. Mining is deeply regulated, geologically complex, and requires extensive physical infrastructure. Moreover, AI may increase demand for silver — the metal is a critical component in solar panels, electronics, and data center infrastructure, all of which benefit from AI-driven growth. Analysts have not flagged AI disruption as a risk for silver miners. The only modest exposure is in back-office operations where AI tools could improve efficiency, but this represents an opportunity rather than a threat. AI Opportunity (0/1): Endeavour Silver does not generate revenue from AI products or services. While AI tools could improve exploration targeting and mine planning efficiency, no specific AI-driven revenue was identified [2].

infinite_game.summary: Endeavour Silver demonstrates elements of infinite thinking through its 45-year corporate history, commitment to community development in its mining regions, and the ICARE Values program that articulates organizational culture. CEO Dan Dickson's focus on safety, culture, and ESG principles alongside profitability shows balanced priorities. However, the company operates in a fundamentally extractive industry where mines have finite lives, and the growth-through-acquisition strategy is more transactional than purpose-driven.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): Endeavour Silver has a genuine cause around responsible mining that creates value for communities, employees, and shareholders. The ICARE Values program and 10-year ESG reporting history demonstrate intentional purpose-building [2][3]. CEO Dickson's emphasis on culture, safety, and sustainability alongside financial performance shows the purpose shapes behavior. However, 'responsible mining' is not a transformative vision — it's an aspiration shared by most modern mining companies. The purpose has not been tested through dramatic crisis or proved by decades of consistent execution under this framework.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): CEO Dan Dickson has led since 2021 with a focus on 'fostering internal culture aligned with ESG principles' and the ICARE Values program [2]. The company operates 2,160 employees across multiple countries with emphasis on workplace safety. Leadership stability since 2021 and the deliberate culture-building suggest adequate trust. However, no specific data on employee turnover, tenure, or cultural satisfaction scores was found.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (0/1): No evidence was found of Endeavour Silver's leadership acknowledging competitors' strengths or using competitive pressure as motivation for self-improvement. The company's competitive positioning focuses on its own production growth and cost metrics rather than learning from peers like First Majestic Silver or Pan American Silver.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): Endeavour Silver has evolved its portfolio through acquisitions — adding the Terronera project (built from exploration to production) and acquiring the Kolpa mine in Peru, expanding from a purely Mexican operation to a pan-Americas miner [1]. These represent meaningful portfolio evolution, though they are incremental growth within the same core business model rather than transformative reinvention.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): Endeavour invested heavily in building the Terronera mine during a period of depressed silver prices, committing capital when near-term returns were uncertain. The Kolpa acquisition in Peru expanded geographic risk but demonstrated willingness to pursue long-term production growth over short-term earnings optimization [1]. These decisions reflect some courage, though they are standard growth-through-development decisions for mid-tier miners.

investment_thesis: Endeavour Silver is at an inflection point following the successful ramp-up of Terronera and acquisition of Kolpa, which together drove Q1 2026 revenue to $210M (up 230% YoY) with $93.5M in mine operating earnings and $112.6M EBITDA [1]. EPS of $0.21 beat consensus estimates by 40%, and 2026 guidance of 14.6-15.6M silver-equivalent ounces provides clear visibility on production growth. At a forward P/E of just 6.1x, the stock appears attractively valued relative to the production transformation underway. The balance sheet is conservatively managed with debt-to-equity of only 39%, and silver's role in solar panels, electronics, and AI-driven infrastructure demand provides a secular tailwind [1]. The company's 10-year ESG reporting track record and ICARE Values program reflect organizational maturity beyond what the stock price reflects.
thesis_continued: The primary risk is that trailing financial metrics still reflect the pre-transformation company — ROE remains negative at -3.9% and profit margin is -3.5%, though forward estimates suggest this reverses sharply in 2026 [1]. Silver price volatility is the dominant risk factor for any silver miner; a retreat from current elevated prices would compress margins regardless of operational execution. The stock has pulled back 47% from its 52-week high of $15.15 to $8.00, which may reflect silver price concerns or profit-taking after the production surge. Mining operations in Mexico and Peru carry geopolitical and regulatory risk, and the Kolpa integration adds execution complexity. At current levels, the stock is priced for continued operational delivery — any production shortfall against the 14.6-15.6M oz guidance would be punished.
key_catalysts: Full-year 2026 production delivery against 14.6-15.6M silver-equivalent ounce guidance, with Terronera and Kolpa reaching steady-state operations and demonstrating sustainable AISC at $27-28/oz
Silver price appreciation driven by solar panel demand growth, AI infrastructure buildout, and potential industrial supply deficit as mine supply growth lags
Transition from negative to positive trailing ROE and profit margin as the full impact of Terronera and Kolpa revenue flows through annual financial statements
key_risks: Silver price decline from current elevated levels would compress margins and could render some production uneconomic, with AISC guidance of $27-28/oz providing limited cushion if silver retreats below $30
Integration risk from the Kolpa acquisition and concurrent Terronera ramp-up strains management attention and capital allocation across three operating countries
Trailing negative ROE (-3.9%) and profit margin (-3.5%) may deter value-oriented investors until annual financials fully reflect the production transformation
